title: South Skyline Trail
subtitle: Ogden Valley Overlook, Utah
summary:
## The Hike In

Two miles up to the Ogden Valley Overlook on the South Skyline Trail. Short approach, big payoff — the ridge sits right on the crest above both Ogden Valley and Eden, with the whole basin spread out below.

## Camp

Dad and Keishin at the top. The overlook lives up to its name: city lights from Ogden on one side, the lights of Eden on the other, all visible from a single ridgeline camp. The kind of spot where you sit outside after dark just to look.

## The Night

The summit usually runs windy — it's exposed on both sides and there's nothing to break it. This time it was completely calm. Lucky break. We sat out without fighting the wind for once, just taking in the view and the quiet.

Dinner was frozen meals cooked up with chicken we'd brought along. Simple, hot, and genuinely good — the kind of meal that tastes better on a mountain than it has any right to.

## Morning

Woke up to the valley still lit in early morning light. The 360° view doesn't get old.

## What Made It

- The calm — this trail earns its reputation for wind, and we got a rare still night
- City lights in every direction from the ridgeline
- Good company, easy miles, and a meal that actually delivered
